3. Vide LVI. 29, 31.

xlvi Brhat Samhitd
commentator quotes not a single statement of Garga or Many. On
the other hand, he quotes extensively K&Syapa, a verse and a half
from Tantrdntara (another scientific work), one line from Visva-
karman and one verse from Maya. Does this mean that the manu-
script of Garga samhitd that was available to Utpala did not contain
this subject ? However, he quotes both the Gargas in the chapter
on architecture (LIII).
(vi) Utpala rightly concludes that the five chapters (LXXIV-
LXXVIH) dealing with 'Praise of Women' and such other'erotic
subjects are meant for the use of royal personages in their harems
[Antafipuracintdydm . .
(vii) He identifies sometimes the author with the work. This
may be intentional, since it is usually asked, "Have you read
Shakespeare ?" meaning his 'works.' In Sanskrit too such a practice
is prevalent. For, Magha is identified with his SiSupalavadha by
